question: How do you know about system memory of a motherboard?
content:
The motherboard system memory section says this board has four 288-pin DDR4 DIMM sockets labeled DIMM_A1, DIMM_A2, DIMM_B1, and DIMM_B2. Use DDR4 memory only; DDR, DDR2, and DDR3 modules must not be installed in these DDR4 slots, and DIMM voltage below 1.65 V is recommended to protect the CPU.<PIC>
It supports 2 GB, 4 GB, 8 GB, and 16 GB unbuffered non-ECC DDR4 DIMMs. For best compatibility, install one DIMM in A2; install two DIMMs in the same-color A2/B2 slots for dual-channel operation; or install four DIMMs in A1/A2 and B1/B2 as two dual-channel pairs.<PIC>
Memory sizes may differ between Channel A and Channel B, but only the lower-sized channel amount runs as dual-channel and the extra memory from the larger channel runs as single-channel. With a 32-bit operating system, 4 GB or more installed memory may leave about 3 GB or less usable, so use up to 3 GB on 32-bit or use a 64-bit operating system for 4 GB or more. For compatibility, use DIMMs with the same CAS latency, preferably the same vendor/version/date code, and use better memory cooling for four DIMMs or overclocking.
ret: "The motherboard system memory section says this board has four 288-pin DDR4 DIMM sockets labeled DIMM_A1, DIMM_A2, DIMM_B1, and DIMM_B2. Use DDR4 memory only; DDR, DDR2, and DDR3 modules must not be installed in these DDR4 slots, and DIMM voltage below 1.65 V is recommended to protect the CPU.<PIC>\nIt supports 2 GB, 4 GB, 8 GB, and 16 GB unbuffered non-ECC DDR4 DIMMs. For best compatibility, install one DIMM in A2; install two DIMMs in the same-color A2/B2 slots for dual-channel operation; or install four DIMMs in A1/A2 and B1/B2 as two dual-channel pairs.<PIC>\nMemory sizes may differ between Channel A and Channel B, but only the lower-sized channel amount runs as dual-channel and the extra memory from the larger channel runs as single-channel. With a 32-bit operating system, 4 GB or more installed memory may leave about 3 GB or less usable, so use up to 3 GB on 32-bit or use a 64-bit operating system for 4 GB or more. For compatibility, use DIMMs with the same CAS latency, preferably the same vendor/version/date code, and use better memory cooling for four DIMMs or overclocking.",["Manual25_18", "Manual25_19"]
notes: Answered from Motherboard section 1.4 System memory; selected the DIMM socket layout and recommended population diagram because the answer names slot labels and installation population.
